summary: Clear, well‑balanced recording captures a solid early‑summer 1983 show highlighted by a sweet “Stranger” opener, jazzy Brent on “Amigo del Diablo,” and an inspired second set.
review: The three reviewers all rave about the performance – a tight first set kicked off by “Stranger,” a second half packed with China > Rider, Playin’ Jam, Spanish Jam and GDTR, plus standout moments like Brent’s jazzy keyboards on “Amigo del Diablo.” The audio is consistently praised as very nice and of good quality, with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are enthusiastic (average 4.3 stars) and highlight both strong musical moments and a clean recording, indicating a high‑quality show with good audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the sound as "very nice" and of "quality recording," noting clear, balanced audio with no reported glitches or muffiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Openers praised for their energy, a second set that flows through extended jams and jazzy keyboard textures; overall pacing described as a hot early‑summer evening that transitioned from daylight into “sweet summer darkness."

band_member_comments.Jerry: Stranger is called "sweet" and his guitar tone is praised; lyrics on Brokedown Palace noted as amazing.
band_member_comments.Phil: "Phile is groovin' on GDTR" highlights a solid bass performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's "cool‑jazz keyboards" on Amigo del Diablo are singled out as transformative.
